# RQ-0810 Execution Plan (Phase 1)

## Task Goal
Reduce CPU overhead in parallel queue selection by caching regexes used by `attempt_json_repair`, while preserving exact repair behavior and replacement ordering, then lock in regressions with focused tests and a low-impact performance visibility check.

## Why
Parallel execution repeatedly calls `load_queue_with_repair_and_validate`, and that function calls JSON repair on malformed queue payloads. `attempt_json_repair` currently compiles the same regexes on each call, which adds avoidable work in lock-held paths. Caching compiled patterns with `LazyLock` removes repeated regex compilation without changing semantics.

## Current Relevant Hot Path
1. `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/worker.rs::select_next_task_locked` always calls `queue::load_queue_with_repair_and_validate` for each selection attempt.
2. `crates/ralph/src/queue.rs::attempt_json_repair` creates four regexes with `regex::Regex::new(...)` every invocation.
3. Replacement ordering is currently:
   1) single-quoted strings, 2) unquoted keys, 3) unescaped newlines, 4) unescaped quotes, 5) trailing commas, 6) trailing commas before newlines, then 7) bracket balance append.

## Phase 2 Implementation Steps

### 1) Cache repair regexes in `crates/ralph/src/queue.rs`
Add module-level lazy regex values and reuse them in-order inside `attempt_json_repair`.

```rust
use regex::Regex;
use std::sync::LazyLock;

static SINGLE_QUOTED_STRING_RE: LazyLock<Regex> =
    LazyLock::new(|| Regex::new(r"(^|[^a-zA-Z0-9])'([^']*?)'([^a-zA-Z0-9]|$)").unwrap());

static UNQUOTED_KEY_RE: LazyLock<Regex> =
    LazyLock::new(|| Regex::new(r"([{,]\s*)([a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*)\s*:").unwrap());

static TRAILING_COMMA_RE: LazyLock<Regex> =
    LazyLock::new(|| Regex::new(r",(\s*[}\]])").unwrap());

static TRAILING_COMMA_NEWLINE_RE: LazyLock<Regex> =
    LazyLock::new(|| Regex::new(r",(\s*)\n(\s*[}\]])").unwrap());
```

Then replace compile-on-call blocks in `attempt_json_repair` with direct static usage.

```rust
repaired = SINGLE_QUOTED_STRING_RE
    .replace_all(&repaired, |caps: &regex::Captures| {
        let prefix = &caps[1];
        let content = &caps[2];
        let suffix = &caps[3];
        let escaped = content.replace('"', "\\\"");
        format!("{}\"{}\"{}", prefix, escaped, suffix)
    })
    .to_string();

repaired = UNQUOTED_KEY_RE
    .replace_all(&repaired, "$1\"$2\":")
    .to_string();

repaired = TRAILING_COMMA_RE.replace_all(&repaired, "$1").to_string();

repaired = TRAILING_COMMA_NEWLINE_RE
    .replace_all(&repaired, "$1\n$2")
    .to_string();
```

Do not alter invocation order, and keep unchanged the subsequent `repair_unescaped_newlines`, `repair_unescaped_quotes`, bracket count logic, and `if repaired != original { Some(repaired) } else { None }` behavior.

### 2) Add ordering-sensitive regression tests in `crates/ralph/src/queue.rs`
Add focused tests near existing repair tests in the same module to guarantee semantics remain identical.

```rust
#[test]
fn attempt_json_repair_preserves_single_quote_then_unquoted_key_order() {
    let input = r#"{'tasks': [{'id': 'RQ-0001', 'title': 'Test'}],}\n"#;
    let repaired = attempt_json_repair(input).expect("should repair");
    assert!(repaired.contains(r#"\"tasks\": [{\"id\": \"RQ-0001\""#));
    let _: QueueFile = serde_json::from_str(&repaired).expect("repaired should parse as JSON");
}

#[test]
fn attempt_json_repair_handles_multiple_ordered_errors() {
    let input = r#"{'version': 1, tasks: [{id: 'RQ-0001', title: 'A', tags: ['bug',],},]}"#;
    let repaired = attempt_json_repair(input).expect("should repair");
    let _parsed: QueueFile = serde_json::from_str(&repaired).expect("repaired should parse as JSON");
    assert!(repaired.contains(r#"\"version\""#));
    assert!(repaired.contains(r#"\"tasks\""#));
    assert!(repaired.contains(r#"\"title\""#));
    assert!(repaired.contains(r#"\"tags\": [\"bug\"]"#));
}
```

### 3) Add low-overhead performance visibility check (non-CI critical)
Use `Instant`-based ignored test/smoke check in `crates/ralph/src/queue.rs` (or a dedicated benches module if preferred) to quantify repeat repair behavior under loop pressure.

```rust
#[test]
#[ignore = "perf-smoke: run manually when tuning hot-path: cargo test -p ralph queue::tests::attempt_json_repair_perf_smoke -- --ignored"]
fn attempt_json_repair_perf_smoke() {
    let input = r#"{'version': 1, tasks: [{'id': 'RQ-0001', 'title': 'A', 'status': 'todo', 'scope': ['x',], 'evidence': ['a',], 'plan': ['x',],}]"#;
    let start = std::time::Instant::now();
    for _ in 0..20_000 {
        let _ = attempt_json_repair(input);
    }
    assert!(start.elapsed().as_millis() >= 0);
}
```

Keep this test non-blocking (`#[ignore]`) unless performance gating is explicitly introduced.

### 4) Validate callsite expectations (no functional change)
Do not change worker selection semantics; keep `select_next_task_locked` behavior intact.

Add/refresh tests in `crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/worker.rs` only if needed to assert repeated repair/selection usage remains correct, reusing existing test style.

```rust
#[test]
fn parallel_select_next_task_locked_still_repairs_without_behavior_change() -> Result<()> {
    // reuse existing resolved path setup, then verify repeated invocations still return same repaired task.
}
```

### 5) Final execution checks
1. `cargo test -p ralph queue::tests`
2. `cargo test -p ralph commands::run::parallel::worker`
3. Optional: run ignored perf smoke intentionally during Phase 2 if available.

### Notes for execution
- If `LazyLock`/`Regex` imports conflict with existing style, resolve by matching existing file conventions (`use` + module-local statics).
- If static regex compilation panics in debug due typo, fix pattern string first in one local unit test before broad execution.
- If exact expected strings in new tests are brittle, validate using `serde_json::from_str` parseability plus targeted contains assertions instead of full deep exact matches.
- If the file layout differs from current snapshot, adapt paths/snippets while preserving the behavior order and shared helpers used by `attempt_json_repair`.
